clusters.title: Sydney schoolgirl speaks out about terrifying bullying incident
clusters.neutral_headline: Sydney schoolgirl speaks out about terrifying bullying incident

items.title: Sydney schoolgirl speaks out about terrifying bullying incident
items.standfirst: A year eight schoolgirl from Sydney has spoken out about a terrifying bullying incident which saw her punched and her head forced into a toilet.
items.content: A year eight schoolgirl from Sydney has spoken out about a terrifying bullying incident which saw her punched and her head forced into a toilet.

items.title: 'Strip naked': Girl, 13, speaks about five-hour school attack
items.standfirst: A Year 8 schoolgirl from Sydney's south has spoken out about a terrifying bullying incident which saw her punched and her head forced into a toilet.
items.content: A Year 8 schoolgirl from Sydney's south has spoken out about a terrifying bullying incident which saw her punched and her head forced into a toilet.The 13-year-old student at Kingsgrove North was subjected to a five-hour bullying ordeal by a group of female students."They started punching me a lot and kicking me," the student, who can not be named, told 9News.LIVE: Trump's right-hand man goes on attack, superpower summit delayed "They also told me to strip naked, which I didn't want to do. "They told me to take your clothes off."The attack, which occurred inside the boys bathroom, was filmed on a mobile phone and shared on social media. READ MORE: Max scrambled to buy his first home before the cash rate became a big problem "They dragged my hair into the toilet bowl, two or three times, they flushed it so many times in there," she said."The boys bathroom, it was already dirty."In one clip a shoe was forced into the student's mouth"The day after that they made me try to kiss their shoe and hit me in the mouth and that made my lip swollen also," she said."I had to kiss both of their feet."READ MORE: NSW government meets with industry leaders as fuel crisis grows Towards the end she was placed into a large rubbish bin."I thought it was going to be my last day on earth," she said."I thought I was gonna die, maybe I gotta say my prayer, maybe this day is coming for me."Three girls aged between 13 and 15 have been charged over the alleged assault.READ MORE: 'We're already cutting back': Anxiety ahead of tomorrow's interest rate hike The girl's mother told 9News she could not watch the full video."Oh my god my heart cries, even now," she said.The victim's mother directed her anger towards the school."I think the school could have done a lot more," she said."I don't think they have done anything, if you can't look after anyone's child, don't take them, don't take them."The Department of Education said in a statement the behaviour is completely unacceptable.An online fundraiser has now raised almost $15,000 dollars for the Year 8 student.Support is available by calling Kids Helpline on 1800 55 1800.NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here
